channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Insurity Expands SI Relationship with Sikich
item.description: Sikich (Chicago), a professional services company specializing in consulting, technology and compliance, has expanded its relationship with Insurity (Hartford), a provider of cloud-based software for insurance carriers, brokers and managing general agents. Through its participation in Insurity’s accelerated Partner Program, Sikich will support insurers seeking to transition from fragmented legacy environments to integrated, cloud-based platforms that support scalability, regulatory responsiveness and growth. As insurers continue to modernize core systems, Sikich is supporting carriers and MGAs as a systems integrator and delivery partner for Insurity’s cloud-based platforms, including Insurity Pro Suite.

item.title: UK Warned of Housing Market Risks as Flood Re’s Future Unclear
item.description: government must provide clarity on the future of Flood Re or risk seeing its failure to do so feed through to the country’s housing market. “It could start to have impacts on housing market decisions for properties that continue to be at flood risk.” The committee is calling for a review that will result in a “publicly announced decision” on the future of Flood Re within the next five years. “One of our top recommendations for government is to undertake a priority review of what is the future of flood reinsurance,” said Richard Millar, the CCC’s director of adaptation.

item.title: Jury Sides With Insurers, Finding Takeda Pharmaceutical Liable for $885 Million in Damages
item.description: jury found Takeda Pharmaceutical liable for causing about $885 million in damages by delaying a generic version of its constipation drug Amitiza through an anticompetitive scheme. Federal antitrust law that covers the bulk of the plaintiffs’ claims allows damages to be automatically tripled, meaning $821.7 million of the jury’s award could swell to $2.47 billion. Monday’s verdict is the first time a jury has found a pharmaceutical company liable in class-action litigation over such pay-for-delay agreements since the Supreme Court’s ruling.

item.title: West Virginia OSHA Safety Inspections Reviewed After Refinery Deaths Last Month
item.description: OSHA, or the Occupational Safety and Health Administration, conducts thousands of workplace inspections every year, including hundreds at workplaces in West Virginia. Because West Virginia does not operate its own OSHA-approved workplace safety program, inspections and enforcement are handled entirely by federal inspectors. Here’s how workplace safety inspections work in West Virginia, and why labor advocates are calling for more oversight to keep workers safe on the job.

item.title: Fears of Unfettered Hacking Spurred by Anthropic’s Mythos AI Model Overblown
item.description: Early fears that Anthropic’s new AI model, Mythos, could dramatically turbocharge hacking are looking overstated a month after its release. But inside the cybersecurity world, the reaction has been more measured — with some saying the broader response has been overblown, and that access to a Mythos-level large language model will not immediately enable hacking operations previously out of reach for bad actors. Organizations’ ability to process and validate a flood of newly discovered vulnerabilities is generally not where it needs to be, the person said, and that is the bigger challenge introduced by Mythos-level models, even as they acknowledged that the model is an improvement.

item.title: Arizona Sheriff Investigates Train/Truck Cargo Theft Associated With California-based Cartel
item.description: Arizona’s Yavapai County Sheriff’s deputies have responded to a string of high-tech cargo burglaries following two separate but similar incidents involving professional theft rings. YCSO, in collaboration with federal agencies, continues to investigate the incidents and is working to identify suspects linked to an organized criminal network based out of California. A semi-truck transporting a high-value load of tech from California made a stop at a truck stop off Interstate 40.

item.title: Tokio Marine’s Greensill reserving fuels US$300m profit drop
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Digital Banking Startup Mercury Lands $200M At $5.2B Valuation Amid Fintech Funding Uptick
item.description: Digital banking startup Mercury has raised $200 million in a Series D round at a $5.2 billion valuation, the company announced Wednesday. I started Mercury because banking should do more than be a vault, it should help customers run the best business possible.” Fintech startups, particularly those that apply AI to traditionally manual or burdensome processes, have benefited from increased investment in recent quarters . That’s up 49% from the $3.5 billion valuation it achieved when announcing its $300 million Series C — which included primary and secondary funding — in March of 2025.

item.title: Florida insurers to benefit from more pronounced June reinsurance renewal softening: AM Best
item.description: Property insurers in the State of Florida are expected to benefit from a more pronounced softening of reinsurance pricing and rates at the June 1 2026 renewals, which may serve to sustain the positive market momentum being seen since the legislative reforms, AM Best has explained. Despite that, alongside improving market conditions and underwriting results, Florida’s property insurers remain among the heaviest users of reinsurance capital, AM Best explained in a new report today. “Despite Florida carriers’ heavy reliance on reinsurance coverage as a risk transfer tool, market reinsurance costs are declining, and capacity is gradually widening as more capital is deployed in the post-reform environment,” AM Best explained.

item.title: One Alliance North America: Debut cat bond supports ability to protect policyholders
item.description: (Series 2026-1) catastrophe bond, has said the upsized transaction supports its ability to protect policyholders and communities. One Alliance North America Insurance Company, the US homeowners and commercial lines specialist underwriter and carrier that recently priced and secured a debut $115 million One Shield Re Ltd. One Alliance North America ventured into the catastrophe bond market to sponsor its first deal in mid-March , with an initial target to secure $100 million or more in multi-peril reinsurance.

item.title: 2026 cyclone season outlook points to fewer hurricanes but more typhoons: Munich Re
item.description: Munich Re meteorologist and climate expert Anja Rädler said the 2026 cyclone season outlook is clearer than in recent years, with El Niño conditions expected to contribute to a slightly weaker Atlantic hurricane season while increasing the likelihood of a more active typhoon season in the western Pacific. “By the end of the year, the phenomenon will likely intensify into a rarely seen “Super El Niño” – a term used to describe events where sea surface temperature anomalies in the equatorial Pacific exceed 2°C, increasing potential global impacts.” Anja Rädler, Meteorologist and climate expert at Munich Re, commented, “Unlike in previous years, the outlook for this cyclone season is relatively clear: given the El Niño conditions, we can expect to see a slightly weaker hurricane season and a stronger typhoon season. On the other hand, conditions are reportedly conducive to the formation of typhoons in the Northwest Pacific, which are more likely to hit Japan, the Greater China region or Korea.

item.title: AM Best reports strong recovery for Florida property insurers as market conditions improve
item.description: According to AM Best, the improvement in market conditions has been supported by a reduction in litigation activity and lower levels of claim solicitation, which has encouraged new insurers to enter the Florida market and enabled existing carriers to recover from earlier losses. AM Best, the international credit rating agency and insurance industry analytics provider, has reported a significant turnaround in the financial performance of Florida-based personal property insurers, with the sector generating close to $1 billion in underwriting profits after recording a $132 million underwriting loss two years earlier. The report also showed that Florida insurers maintain considerably higher levels of ceded reinsurance leverage than the broader US personal property market.

item.title: Prismic closes oversubscribed capital raise to support wider range of reinsurance opportunities
item.description: Prismic said the additional capital expands its capacity and flexibility to support a broader range of reinsurance opportunities. The capital raise included investments from a global consortium of institutional investors, underscoring continued demand for Prismic’s differentiated reinsurance platform. Following the latest close, Prismic has raised more than $3.3 billion in capital to support reinsurance liabilities, including Prudential Financial’s U.S.

item.title: Markets/Coverages: BHSI Offers New Casualty Insurance Policies in Switzerland
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway Specialty Insurance (BHSI) announced the launch of two new casualty insurance policies in Switzerland: one multinational and another for domestic risks, both tailored for large corporate and upper middle market commercial and industrial organizations in Switzerland. “These new casualty policies mark the next step in building out our lead product capabilities in Switzerland,” said Franco Masciovecchio, country manager, Switzerland, BHSI. “We look forward to continuing to expand our team, our product lines and our relationships with brokers, customers, and business partners in Switzerland.” In addition to casualty insurance, BHSI’s capabilities in Switzerland include property damage and business interruption insurance, Executive First D&O Liability Insurance for commercial and industrial organizations, financial institutions and investment managers, and multinational insurance programs.

item.title: People Moves: Liberty Names Kooijman as GM, Insurance, Netherlands, Belgium, Nordics
item.description: Liberty Taps Kooijman as General Manager, Insurance, Netherlands, Belgium, Nordics Liberty Specialty Markets , part of Liberty Mutual Insurance Group, announced the appointment of Monique Kooijman as general manager for Netherlands, Belgium and the Nordics, subject to regulatory approval. Kooijman will be based in the Netherlands and will report to Pierre-Edouard Fraigneau , president, Liberty Europe. Kooijman joins Liberty from Everest, where she served as head of Northern Europe, playing a key role in accelerating regional growth and strengthening strategic broker relationships across the market.

item.title: ‘Am I out?’ Drought and Rising Costs From Iran War Deepen Pain for US Farmers
item.description: Soaring costs of fuel and commercial fertilizer in the wake of the Iran war are making hard times worse for farmers across the U.S. Even before the war, farmers were struggling with a resurgent drought, high input costs, and the fallout from President Donald Trump’s trade policies, which hobbled export markets and drove down prices for their crops. And yet, because of the drought, farmers are looking at the prospect of smaller harvests to pay for it all.

item.title: Ascend and Honor Capital merge to create integrated insurance finance platform
item.description: Ascend has agreed to merge with premium finance provider Honor Capital in a deal aimed at creating what the companies describe as the insurance industry’s first fully vertically integrated financial operations platform. “Together with Ascend, we are defining a new category for insurance financial operations by combining AI-powered accounting automation, payments, and premium finance into the first complete platform experience built for the insurance industry.” Ascend co-president Andrew Wynn said, “We started Ascend because insurance businesses deserve better software and tools. Ascend’s platform is currently used by more than 4,000 insurance businesses, including more than half of the 50 largest brokers in the US.

item.title: InsurGrid Expands AI-Powered Policy Extraction and Comparison for P&C Agents
item.description: The post InsurGrid Expands AI-Powered Policy Extraction and Comparison for P&C Agents appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . SAN FRANCISCO, CA — May 19, 2026 — InsurGrid , an AI insurance technology company and the leading policy data platform for P&C insurance agents, today announces expansion of its AI-powered Policy Extraction and Policy Comparison tools. Now agents upload both documents and have the answer in under a minute — every coverage line, every dollar difference, every gap.” — Rohit Mittal , CEO, InsurGrid Key Features Policy Extraction — AI reads any carrier’s policy PDF and converts it into structured coverage data, handling personal and commercial lines across hundreds of carrier formats Side-by-Side Comparison — Generates formatted comparison tables showing coverage changes, premium impacts, and gaps between an existing policy and a new quote Gap and Upsell Detection — Surfaces missing coverages and underinsured areas that are easy to miss in manual reviews, with early users reporting 73% more upsell opportunities identified per client Customer Impact “What used to take me 45 minutes per client now takes less than a minute.

item.title: Kin names Douglas Everson chief product officer
item.description: Kin Insurance named Douglas Everson chief product officer, where he will oversee product strategy across the company’s insurance, financing, and homeownership offerings. We’re still early in that journey.” – Kim Chief Product Officer Douglas Everson. Everson, who joined Kin in 2023, will lead the company’s Product Management and Design teams and manage the roadmap for home insurance, auto insurance, home financing, and future homeownership services.

item.title: Liberty Mutual launches life sciences insurance solution for middle market companies
item.description: The post Liberty Mutual launches life sciences insurance solution for middle market companies appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Liberty Mutual Insurance Global Risk Solutions launched Liberty Remedy for Life Sciences, a multi-line insurance product designed for middle market life sciences companies. Liberty Remedy reflects that commitment to partnership — on both offense and defense — helping clients proactively mitigate risk, address complex challenges, and deliver strong protection when it matters most.” – Tory Agnich, Liberty Mutual chief underwriting officer, GRS Middle Market Life Sciences.

item.title: Munich Re warns of ‘turbulent typhoon’ season in North Asia as ‘Super El Niño’ risk looms
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Reduced nat cat losses, higher premiums propel MS&AD’s FY25 net income to US$5bn
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Tokio Marine adjusted profit rises 17% to US$4.4bn on underwriting strength, lower nat cat losses
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA opens doors to support fast-growing financial firms
item.description: Fast‑growing and innovative financial services businesses can now apply for more support to help them grow. This has provided the FCA insight into how it can best support growing firms and will continue this dialogue with solo-regulated firms. The FCA’s Scale-up Unit provides tailored support to firms, helping them navigate regulation so they can scale sustainably.

item.title: Embodied AI Fuels Record Robotics Funding In China As IPO Momentum Builds
item.description: A review of Crunchbase data shows that investors now are no longer funding mostly pre-programmed hardware, but increasingly backing China-based startups working on embodied AI — or artificial intelligence with a physical body that interacts with the real world in real time. In China, robotics startups at the intersection of the software and hardware integration are drawing the largest checks in the space and often back-to-back funding rounds. Venture investment in China’s robotics sector has hit an all-time high this year, Crunchbase data shows, as several well-funded startups in the space make IPO debuts.

item.title: After A 6-Figure Fertility Journey, This Founder Built An IVF Startup With ‘Outcome Protection’
item.description: “No one else today bundles care, capital and financial protection into a single product.” And today, the New York-based startup — led by AlSalim as its sole founder — tells Crunchbase News exclusively that it has secured a $100 million debt facility from Viola Credit to scale its operations across the United States. But after finally achieving a successful outcome, AlSalim recognized how different his experience was from many others in his position. Five cycles, three clinics, two countries and a six-figure financial toll spanning about four years.

item.title: Lockton Re unveils dedicated ILS platform for Brazil
item.description: Lockton Re, the reinsurance arm of the independent broking group, has announced the full deployment of its advisory and structuring capabilities dedicated to Brazil’s Letra de Risco de Seguro (LRS /Insurance Risk Letters) market, utilising locally licensed Special Purpose Securitisation Entities (SSPE). The firm’s dedicated Brazilian ILS platform reportedly accommodates a wide range of triggers, including indemnity, parametric, and index-based structures, spanning perils such as agribusiness, infrastructure, climate catastrophe and regulatory capital relief. Lockton Re’s Brazilian ILS platform is supported by a highly skilled leadership team with extensive knowledge across the ILS sector.

item.title: New York metro has largest exposure concentration to hurricane risk in US: Cotality
item.description: According to research data from Cotality, a global provider of property information, analytics and technology-enabled data solutions, the New York metropolitan area has the largest concentration of homes exposed to hurricane-related risks in the United States. In the firm’s recently published 2026 Hurricane Risk Report, Cotality estimates that more than 3.27 million homes across New York face moderate or greater levels of hurricane wind risk. Cotality also observed that New York also leads the United States in storm surge exposure, with more than 631,000 homes said to be at risk and roughly $329 billion in RCV.

item.title: Ariel SSPE launching as third Brazilian insurance-linked securities structure
item.description: (Ariel SSPE) will be the third insurance-linked securities (ILS) structure in Brazil, harnessing the country’s regulations to become an independent issuer of Letra de Risco de Seguro (Insurance Risk Letters), Brazil’s own brand of ILS instrument. Meza took technical leadership for the structuring and issuance of the Andrina SSPE and promoted the adoption of insurance-linked securities techniques within the reinsurance company, during her career at IRB (Re). Ariel SSPE is set to be led by co-founder Aline Meza, who previously worked at IRB (Re) and led much of the effort for the Andrina SSPE structure and its first LRS issuance.

item.title: Tokio Marine’s International business sees profit rise to JPY 473.9bn in FY’25
item.description: Tokio Marine Holdings has announced its full year results for the 2025 fiscal year, reporting a 9.5% (excluding FX effects) year on year increase in its International business profits, rising to JPY473.9 billion from JPY428.4 billion. Overall, the non-life segment saw a 2025 profit of JPY489.2 billion in FY 2025, a 5.4% year on year increase. Regionally, North America was a major profit driver, surging 18% year on year to profit of JPY428.4 billion.

item.title: Gallagher names Adam Stafford as COO of Specialty division
item.description: Gallagher, an insurance brokerage, risk management and consulting firm, has appointed Adam Stafford as Chief Operating Officer (COO) for its Specialty division, thereby expanding its London Market operational expertise. In his new role, Stafford will report to Sarah Lyons, Chief Executive Officer, Gallagher Specialty and will become part of the broker’s operations leadership team led by Charles Crawford, COO, UK & EMEA. Lyons commented on the appointment, “Adam is the ideal individual to take the helm of our specialty operations.

item.title: Distinguished Programs launches Marine Cargo programme with Allianz backing
item.description: Distinguished Programs, a national MGA and insurance programme manager, has officially opened its Marine Cargo programme for business. Ralph Salce, President of Distinguished Marine Cargo, is leading the programme, bringing more than three decades of industry experience. Distinguished noted that the Marine Cargo programme further expands its portfolio of specialty offerings, which includes environmental, fine art and collectibles, real estate, hospitality, restaurant, transactional liability, community association, executive lines, surety, and inland marine.

item.title: Arthur J. Gallagher’s RPS Acquires McKee Risk Management
item.description: (RPS) reports it has acquired McKee Risk Management, Inc., a King of Prussia, Pennsylvania-based program administrator. McKee Risk Management provides underwriting, policy administration, claims coordination and risk management services with program focuses of construction, public entity and property. “McKee Risk Management brings a well-established platform and underwriting expertise that complement RPS’s programs offerings,” said J.

item.title: Iran Consolidating Control of Hormuz With Checkpoints, Vetting and Sometimes ‘Fees’
item.description: With Iran now in de facto control of the strait, the system can involve government-to-government arrangements, intense vetting by the Iranian government and sometimes fees in exchange for safe passage, Reuters has found. The tanker crew gathered their courage and carefully navigated along a route designated by Iran, hugging the coastline and maneuvering their hulking vessel between island checkpoints through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran’s orders to the tanker were part of a complex, multi-tiered mechanism that the country has deployed for clearing vessels through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Murdaugh Sues Clerk Whose Actions Led to Overturning His Murder Conviction
item.description: Alex Murdaugh has filed a lawsuit against the court clerk whose behavior during his murder trial led the South Carolina Supreme Court to overturn his convictions and life sentence for the deaths of his wife and son. The suit filed Sunday in federal court accuses former Colleton County Clerk of Court Rebecca Hill of violating Murdaugh’s right to a fair trial and seeks punitive and compensatory damages and attorneys’ fees. South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson, whose office prosecuted Murdaugh, said in a news release that the “Becky Hill matter was previously referred to and reviewed by an independent prosecutor.” Griffin said Murdaugh got emotional when they talked about the Supreme Court’s decision.

item.title: Liberty Mutual tests ChatGPT integration for auto insurance quotes
item.description: Liberty Mutual has been testing a ChatGPT integration that enables users to request auto insurance quote estimates directly within the ChatGPT interface, signalling a potential new distribution channel for insurance shopping. The onboarding flow described the experience as a Liberty Mutual-powered tool inside ChatGPT built to deliver “fast, personalized auto insurance quote estimates” using limited customer information. While quote estimates were surfaced within ChatGPT, policy purchases, issuance, customer support, and claims handling remained within Liberty Mutual’s own platforms.

item.title: HDI Global appoints Thomas Kropp as COO/CIO and elevates AI under CEO in Xcelerate29 reshuffle
item.description: At the same time, we are committed to our services and taking them to the next level, with the ambition of supporting clients and brokers effectively as a reliable partner.” The post HDI Global appoints Thomas Kropp as COO/CIO and elevates AI under CEO in Xcelerate29 reshuffle appeared first on Insurtech Insights . HDI Global has appointed Thomas Kropp as chief operating officer and chief information officer (COO/CIO), succeeding Dr. Thomas Kuhnt, as the corporate and specialty insurer restructures executive responsibilities under its Xcelerate29 strategy.

item.title: Coterie appoints Brett Saternus as chief underwriting officer
item.description: Coterie Insurance announced the appointment of Brett Saternus as Chief Underwriting Officer. “Underwriting discipline is foundational to how Coterie creates value for agents, carriers, and small business owners. Saternus brings more than 15 years of experience across specialty carriers, insurtech MGAs, and commercial insurance.

item.title: Porch Group launches insurance in Michigan
item.description: Porch Group announced that Homeowners of America has launched in Michigan, marking the 22nd state in which HOA operates. This launch supports Porch’s broader strategy to expand distribution and drive premium growth and cash flow over time through its insurance platform. “We’re pleased to bring HOA’s homeowners insurance offering to Michigan and expand our ability to serve independent agents and policyholders in another important market.

item.title: Progressive reports 90.2% CR for April
item.description: Progressive released its financial results for April, ending the month with a combined ratio of 90.2% and net income of ~$1.1 billion. Year-to-date, Progressive’s net income stands at $3.9 billion and combined ratio sits at 87.4%. The insurer had ~39.7 million policies at the end of April, an increase of 8% compared to April 2025.

item.title: Es inoponible la cláusula de carencia de 90 días en un seguro de Vida por infarto de miocardio
item.description: La Sala Primera del Tribunal Supremo ha resuelto que la cláusula que establece un período de carencia de 90 días para la cobertura del infarto de miocardio en un seguro de Vida temporal constituye una cláusula limitativa de los derechos del asegurado y no una mera delimitación del riesgo. Al no cumplir los requisitos formales del artículo 3 de la Ley de Contrato de Seguro (LCS), la cláusula resulta inoponible al asegurado. En una reciente sentencia, comentada en Revista RC , el Supremo reitera la doctrina fijada en la sentencia 1321/2023, de 27 de septiembre, sobre cláusulas de carencia en seguros de Vida.

item.title: Compliance en seguros: valor estratégico en un entorno regulado
item.description: Desde mi experiencia, el verdadero valor de Compliance no reside únicamente en supervisar el cumplimiento normativo. Mantenerla exige solvencia financiera, excelencia operativa y, cada vez más, una gobernanza sólida capaz de responder a un entorno regulatorio en constante evolución. En este contexto, la función de Compliance ha adquirido un papel central dentro de las entidades aseguradoras .

item.title: Olga Sánchez: «Axa se encuentra en la mejor posición que hemos tenido nunca»
item.description: Asimismo, señaló que la aseguradora se encuentra «en la mejor posición que hemos tenido nunca», gracias a «una visión estratégica clara, las mejores herramientas y personas que hacen que la compañía avance con una clara ejecución de los proyectos». El acto contó también con la participación de Olga Sánchez, consejera delegada de Axa España, quien intervino en una mesa redonda junto a todos los miembros del Comité Ejecutivo de la compañía. En este sentido, Mar Romero, directora de Distribución, Ventas y Organización Territorial (en la imagen que acompaña a esta información) , destacó la fortaleza de la estructura comercial: « Tenemos el mejor equipo comercial, la mejor red y los mejores productos «.

item.title: Mutual of Enumclaw Names Peterson Chief Claims Officer
item.description: Mutual of Enumclaw named Joe Peterson vice president of claims and chief claims officer. Peterson has more than 20 years of claims leadership experience, most recently as vice president – P/C claims at Zenith Insurance Company. Mutual of Enumclaw is a Washington-based regional insurer specializing in personal, business and farm and ranch coverage.

item.title: Wall Street Watchdogs Pause Some Cyber Exams After Mythos Shock
item.description: In April, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and then-Fed Chair Jerome Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to warn them of the cyber risks raised by AI models such as Mythos. regulators are pausing some cyber-related examinations of the largest banks, giving the lenders more breathing room as the firms dig into the risks exposed by Anthropic PBC’s new Mythos AI model. Anthropic said last month that it would limit access to Mythos, warning that it could potentially be used to power cyber attacks.

item.title: Openly’s Tech-Forward Approach Includes AI in Claims
item.description: Openly, which promotes itself as a tech-forward homeowners insurance provider, has been making a name for itself by enabling agents to generate quotes in times as little as 15 seconds since being founded in 2017. The Boston, Massachusetts-based insurtech, which operates in 19 states and counting, continues to embrace that tech-forward mantra by adopting artificial intelligence in its claims process. To get a good handle what the company is doing with AI, Claims Journal spoke with April Stone, senior director of claims at Openly.

item.title: Aon Malaysia confirms double leadership promotions
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Zurich elevates Kin Lee to APAC head of energy

item.title: MS Amlin posts 30% jump in insurance service result, COR down to 83%

item.title: Young drivers warned about fake insurance sold on social media
item.description: Half (49%) of young drivers have bought insurance through social media or messaging apps, new research reveals. The FCA is warning 17-to 25-year-old drivers about 'ghost broking' scams where criminals sell bogus insurance policies through social media and messaging platforms. Check the FCA Firm Checker before you buy, because driving uninsured could cost you far more than any premium.' The FCA is working with social media influencers to warn young drivers about the growing threat of ghost broking.
